hypothesis: If E2EFOLD is trained end-to-end with the composite loss (FAPE, Cα-FAPE, torsion, distogram, and sequence-recovery) and uses discrete sequence design via Gumbel-Softmax, then the designed sequences will achieve high sequence recovery and the predicted all-atom structures will closely match reference structures (high TM-score and low RMSD) on held-out datasets, outperforming Boltz-2 and standard baselines because joint optimization couples sequence design with accurate structure reconstruction.
